definitions: adj. 极度渴望的；非常想要的（常接 to do 或 for sth）
adj. 绝望的；危急的；不顾一切的

examples.text: We still tell stories, are hungry for food, greedy for resources, desperate to be accepted by our peers.
examples.explanation: 这里的 desperate to be accepted 表示“极度渴望被同伴接纳”。desperate 后常接 to do，强调一种很强烈、近乎迫切的愿望。

examples.text: Desperate, the Jade Emperor called on the only being stronger than he, the supreme power in all the heavens: the Buddha himself.
examples.explanation: 这里的 Desperate 表示“走投无路的、绝望的”。玉皇大帝因为局势危急，只能求助于比自己更强大的佛陀。

source.text: The tale of the Monkey King and the Buddha - Ji Hao

reviewSessions.example_text: to survive. We are all still bound to our nature that made us so successful. We still tell stories, are hungry for food, greedy for resources, desperate to be accepted by our peers. We are scared by the dangers that lurk in the dark, imagined and real ones. We are still brutal to each other and the animals we hold power over. We are still territorial and possessive, we fear losing what we have, and we fear
reviewSessions.feedback: 这次没填对，正确答案是 desperate。
missing_points: 需要回忆出目标词 desperate

relations.reason: 两者都常接不定式表达意愿强弱，但语义方向相反：reluctant to do 表示“不情愿做”，desperate to do 表示“极度想做”。这种对照有明确学习价值，便于掌握态度强弱的反义关系。
